title: Australia's Political System
shortSummary: Learn the operational map of Australian politics: voters, House, Senate, Cabinet, ministers, the APS, courts, states, and the accountability layer around them.
whyItMatters: Australia is the main worked example for this subject. Without a clear power map, Parliament looks like theatre instead of a real operating system.
coreConcepts: House of Representatives and government formation
mentalModels: House forms government, Senate makes negotiation real
Cabinet is the political decision core

goDeeper.label: See the politics system map
goDeeper.description: Use the visual map if the institutional picture still feels abstract.

goDeeper.label: Continue into elections and representation
goDeeper.description: Move from structure into how people actually win and hold power.
